user_story: As an operator, I want to check whether the local PMXT server is alive so that I can route traffic only to healthy instances.
expected_behavior: GET /health returns a successful health payload without exchange credentials.

user_story: As a developer, I want to load and cache markets from an exchange so that repeated iteration does not drift across API calls.
expected_behavior: POST /api/{exchange}/loadMarkets loads all markets into markets and marketsBySlug and returns the cached map on later calls unless reload is requested.

user_story: As a market-data user, I want to fetch live markets with filters so that I can discover current tradable questions.
expected_behavior: GET /api/{exchange}/fetchMarkets accepts filters such as query, limit, status, slug, marketId, outcomeId, eventId, category, tags, sourceExchange, and returns live UnifiedMarket rows.
observed_behavior: Mock exchange GET and POST fetchMarkets parity passed for limit=3; broader live venue/filter coverage still pending.

user_story: As a market-data user, I want stable market pagination so that I can page through a snapshot without result drift.
expected_behavior: GET /api/{exchange}/fetchMarketsPaginated creates or reuses an in-memory snapshot, returns data, total, and nextCursor, and slices later pages from the same snapshot.

user_story: As a market-data user, I want stable event pagination so that event pages stay consistent while I browse.
expected_behavior: GET /api/{exchange}/fetchEventsPaginated creates or reuses an event snapshot and returns data, total, and nextCursor.

user_story: As a market-data user, I want to fetch events with optional search so that I can browse grouped markets.
expected_behavior: GET /api/{exchange}/fetchEvents returns UnifiedEvent rows and supports filters including query, limit, status, eventId, slug, series, category, tags, and sourceExchange.
observed_behavior: Mock exchange GET and POST fetchEvents parity passed for limit=3; broader live venue/filter coverage still pending.

user_story: As a market-data user, I want to fetch recurring series when venues expose them so that I can navigate the Series to Event to Market hierarchy.
expected_behavior: GET /api/{exchange}/fetchSeries returns venue series for supported venues and an empty array for venues without a series concept.

user_story: As a developer, I want a single-market lookup so that I do not have to filter arrays myself.
expected_behavior: GET /api/{exchange}/fetchMarket wraps fetchMarkets and returns exactly one UnifiedMarket or throws MarketNotFound when no match exists.

user_story: As a developer, I want a single-event lookup so that I can load one grouped event by id, slug, or query.
expected_behavior: GET /api/{exchange}/fetchEvent wraps fetchEvents and returns exactly one UnifiedEvent or throws EventNotFound when no match exists.

user_story: As a charting user, I want OHLCV candles for an outcome so that I can plot historical price movement.
expected_behavior: GET /api/{exchange}/fetchOHLCV returns PriceCandle rows for an outcome using a required resolution plus optional start, end, and limit.
observed_behavior: Mock exchange GET and POST fetchOHLCV parity passed for resolution=1h, limit=5, and date-time start/end coercion; live venue coverage still pending.

user_story: As a trader, I want an order book for an outcome so that I can inspect bids, asks, and available depth.
expected_behavior: GET /api/{exchange}/fetchOrderBook returns bids, asks, and timestamp for live books and supports historical snapshots or ranges where implemented.
observed_behavior: Mock exchange GET and POST fetchOrderBook parity passed for bids, asks, prices, sizes, and timestamps; historical/live venue coverage still pending.

user_story: As a trader, I want order books for multiple outcomes in one request so that I can compare related prices efficiently.
expected_behavior: POST /api/{exchange}/fetchOrderBooks returns a map from outcome id to OrderBook for supported venues.

user_story: As a market-data user, I want raw trade history for an outcome so that I can analyze executed market activity.
expected_behavior: GET /api/{exchange}/fetchTrades returns Trade rows and accepts optional start, end, and limit parameters.

user_story: As a trader, I want to place an order through a unified method so that I can trade across venues with one interface.
expected_behavior: POST /api/{exchange}/createOrder validates required order fields, supports buy or sell and market or limit types where supported, and returns a normalized Order.
observed_behavior: Validation path passed for missing required order fields; successful order placement still pending because it needs venue/mock trade-flow coverage.

user_story: As a trader or middleware, I want to build an order without submitting it so that I can inspect, sign, or forward it later.
expected_behavior: POST /api/{exchange}/buildOrder returns a BuiltOrder payload without placing the order.

user_story: As a trader, I want to submit a previously built order so that signing and submission can be separated.
expected_behavior: POST /api/{exchange}/submitOrder accepts a BuiltOrder from buildOrder and returns the resulting normalized Order.

user_story: As a trader, I want to cancel an open order so that I can remove resting liquidity.
expected_behavior: POST /api/{exchange}/cancelOrder cancels an existing order by id and returns the normalized Order result or a venue-specific not-supported error.

user_story: As a trader, I want to fetch one order by id so that I can check its latest state.
expected_behavior: GET /api/{exchange}/fetchOrder returns a normalized Order for supported venues or a not-supported error.

user_story: As a trader, I want to list open orders so that I can monitor resting orders.
expected_behavior: GET /api/{exchange}/fetchOpenOrders returns open Order rows, optionally filtered by market id.

user_story: As an authenticated trader, I want my fill history so that I can reconcile account activity.
expected_behavior: GET /api/{exchange}/fetchMyTrades returns UserTrade rows with optional market, outcome, since, until, limit, or cursor filters where supported.

user_story: As a trader, I want closed order history so that I can audit completed or cancelled orders.
expected_behavior: GET /api/{exchange}/fetchClosedOrders returns closed Order rows where the venue supports historical orders.

user_story: As a trader, I want all open and closed orders so that I can reconstruct order history.
expected_behavior: GET /api/{exchange}/fetchAllOrders returns combined order history where supported.

user_story: As an account user, I want current positions so that I can see my exposure across markets.
expected_behavior: GET /api/{exchange}/fetchPositions returns Position rows for the configured account or supplied address where supported.

user_story: As an account user, I want balances so that I know funds available for trading.
expected_behavior: GET /api/{exchange}/fetchBalance returns Balance rows for the configured account or supplied address where supported.
observed_behavior: Mock exchange GET and POST fetchBalance parity passed; authenticated live venue coverage still pending.

user_story: As a trader, I want the average execution price for a target size so that I can estimate slippage.
expected_behavior: POST /api/{exchange}/getExecutionPrice computes volume-weighted average price from an OrderBook and returns 0 when the requested size cannot fully fill.

user_story: As a trader, I want fill details for an execution estimate so that I can understand partial fill behavior and levels consumed.
expected_behavior: POST /api/{exchange}/getExecutionPriceDetailed returns detailed fill and partial-fill information from an OrderBook, side, and amount.

user_story: As a developer, I want to filter market arrays locally so that I can apply text and structured criteria after fetching.
expected_behavior: POST /api/{exchange}/filterMarkets filters provided markets by string query, structured criteria, or a custom filter function where available.

user_story: As a developer, I want to filter event arrays locally so that I can apply text and structured criteria after fetching.
expected_behavior: POST /api/{exchange}/filterEvents filters provided events by string query, structured criteria, or a custom filter function where available.

user_story: As an application operator, I want to close exchange resources so that WebSocket and network handles are released cleanly.
expected_behavior: POST /api/{exchange}/close closes WebSocket connections and other resources for the exchange instance.

user_story: As a cross-venue user, I want to find equivalent or related markets on other venues so that I can compare opportunities.
expected_behavior: GET /api/router/fetchMarketMatches supports lookup mode and browse mode, returning MatchResult rows from the hosted match catalog or local mock handling.
observed_behavior: Local mock market lookup returns [] when mock exchange is configured, throws clear unsupported error without mock exchange, sidecar resolves bundled mock IDs locally, and malformed hosted envelope errors are detected; real hosted catalog coverage pending.

user_story: As a cross-venue user, I want to find equivalent or related events on other venues so that I can compare grouped markets.
expected_behavior: GET /api/router/fetchEventMatches supports lookup mode and browse mode, returning EventMatchResult rows.
observed_behavior: Local mock event lookup returns [] when mock exchange is configured, sidecar resolves bundled mock IDs locally, and malformed hosted envelope errors are detected; real hosted catalog coverage pending.

user_story: As a trader, I want live price comparisons for matched markets so that I can spot venue differences.
expected_behavior: POST /api/router/compareMarketPrices returns side-by-side best bid and ask data for identity matches.

user_story: As a researcher, I want subset or superset related markets so that I can reason about hedges and implications.
expected_behavior: GET /api/router/fetchRelatedMarkets returns related PriceComparison rows across venues.

user_story: As a cross-venue user, I want matched market pairs so that I can inspect catalog relationships.
expected_behavior: GET /api/router/fetchMatchedMarkets returns MatchedMarketPair rows from the router catalog.

user_story: As a trader, I want matched market prices so that I can compare prices for catalog-paired markets.
expected_behavior: GET /api/router/fetchMatchedPrices returns MatchedPricePair rows.

user_story: As a trader, I want hedging opportunities so that I can offset exposure across related markets.
expected_behavior: GET /api/router/fetchHedges returns PriceComparison rows for hedge-like related markets.

user_story: As a trader, I want arbitrage opportunities so that I can find crossed or mispriced matched markets.
expected_behavior: GET /api/router/fetchArbitrage returns ArbitrageOpportunity rows.

user_story: As a data-feed user, I want to list available feed providers so that I know what external market data can be queried.
expected_behavior: GET /api/feeds returns the available feed names, currently binance and chainlink.

user_story: As a data-feed user, I want feed markets or symbols so that I can know which symbols the feed supports.
expected_behavior: GET /api/feeds/{feed}/loadMarkets returns all trading pairs or feeds supported by the selected provider.

user_story: As a data-feed user, I want one latest ticker so that I can read current external reference prices.
expected_behavior: GET /api/feeds/{feed}/fetchTicker requires symbol and returns the latest ticker for that symbol.
observed_behavior: Backend error path passed: binance fetchTicker returns 503 with BINANCE_RELAY_WS_URL named when relay config is missing; successful feed fetch still pending.

user_story: As a data-feed user, I want many latest tickers so that I can monitor several symbols efficiently.
expected_behavior: GET /api/feeds/{feed}/fetchTickers returns all latest tickers or a subset from a comma-separated symbols query.

user_story: As a charting user, I want feed OHLCV candles so that I can plot external price history.
expected_behavior: GET /api/feeds/{feed}/fetchOHLCV requires symbol, accepts timeframe, since, and limit, and returns candle rows where supported.
observed_behavior: Backend capability error path passed: binance OHLCV returns route-level 501 unsupported response; supported-feed success path still pending.

user_story: As a data-feed user, I want an external order book so that I can inspect non-prediction-market liquidity.
expected_behavior: GET /api/feeds/{feed}/fetchOrderBook requires symbol and returns an order book where the feed supports it or 501 otherwise.
observed_behavior: Backend capability error path passed: binance order book returns route-level 501 unsupported response; supported-feed success path still pending.

user_story: As an oracle-data user, I want the latest Chainlink round so that I can inspect on-chain reference data.
expected_behavior: GET /api/feeds/{feed}/fetchOracleRound requires feed and returns the latest oracle round where supported.
observed_behavior: Backend error path passed: chainlink oracle route returns 503 with CHAINLINK_API_URL named when config is missing; successful oracle fetch still pending.

user_story: As an oracle-data user, I want historical Chainlink rounds so that I can audit price feed history.
expected_behavior: GET /api/feeds/{feed}/fetchOracleHistory requires feed and optional limit, returning historical oracle rounds where supported.

user_story: As a data-feed user, I want historical feed prices over a range so that I can backfill analysis.
expected_behavior: GET /api/feeds/{feed}/fetchHistoricalPrices requires symbol and accepts fromTimestamp, untilTimestamp, maxSize, and order.

user_story: As a streaming user, I want live feed ticker updates so that my app can react to price changes.
expected_behavior: WebSocket subscribe with targetKind=feed and method=watchTicker sends subscribed and data events until unsubscribe or close.

user_story: As a streaming trader, I want live order book updates for one outcome so that my UI can stay current.
expected_behavior: WebSocket subscribe with targetKind=exchange and method=watchOrderBook loops exchange.watchOrderBook and emits data events, or an error event on fatal failure.

user_story: As a streaming trader, I want batched live order book updates so that I can watch related outcomes together.
expected_behavior: WebSocket subscribe with method=watchOrderBooks emits per-symbol data events from the returned record.

user_story: As a streaming trader, I want live trade updates so that I can monitor market prints.
expected_behavior: WebSocket subscribe with method=watchTrades loops exchange.watchTrades and emits trade data events.

user_story: As a hosted streaming user, I want a firehose of order-book updates so that I can consume multiple venues from one stream.
expected_behavior: SDK watchAllOrderBooks subscribes to the hosted websocket and returns FirehoseEvent updates filtered by optional venues.

user_story: As a hosted API user, I want clustered matched events so that I can browse cross-venue event relationships without flattening to pairs.
expected_behavior: GET /v0/matched-event-clusters returns connected clusters of semantically matched events across venues.

user_story: As a hosted API user, I want clustered matched markets so that I can browse cross-venue market relationships without flattening to pairs.
expected_behavior: GET /v0/matched-market-clusters returns connected clusters of semantically matched markets across venues.

user_story: As an enterprise user, I want read-only SQL access so that I can query PMXT data safely.
expected_behavior: POST /v0/sql executes read-only ClickHouse SQL and rejects unsafe or mutating statements.
observed_behavior: SQL route accepted hosted POST body and GET query shapes, rejected missing and write queries before config checks, enforced enterprise plan, and proxied read-only SQL to fake ClickHouse with readonly limits.

user_story: As a hosted trader, I want to build an order before submitting so that a wallet can review and sign typed data.
expected_behavior: POST /v0/trade/build-order returns typed_data, a built_order_id, economics, and order preview without placing the trade.

user_story: As a hosted trader, I want to submit a signed built order so that the trade is placed after explicit wallet approval.
expected_behavior: POST /v0/trade/submit-order accepts built_order_id and signature before expiry and returns the resulting Order.

user_story: As a hosted trader, I want one-shot order creation so that the SDK can build, sign, and submit a trade for me.
expected_behavior: POST /v0/trade/create-order places a buy or sell order on supported hosted venues and returns id, status, fill data, fees, and tx hash when available.

user_story: As a hosted trader, I want to start a cancel flow so that a wallet can sign the cancel payload before it is submitted.
expected_behavior: POST /v0/orders/cancel/build returns cancel typed data for an existing open order; the SDK cancelOrder chains build, sign, and submit.

user_story: As a hosted trader, I want escrow balance so that I know available PMXT trading funds.
expected_behavior: GET /v0/user/{address}/balances returns the wallet balance inside PMXT escrow rather than wallet on-chain USDC.

user_story: As a hosted trader, I want open positions across hosted venues so that I can see my exposure.
expected_behavior: GET /v0/user/{address}/positions returns positions with market, outcome, share count, and average entry price; mark-to-market fields may be null.

user_story: As a hosted trader, I want open hosted orders so that I can monitor resting orders.
expected_behavior: GET /v0/orders/open lists wallet orders that have not filled or cancelled and supports venue filtering.

user_story: As a hosted trader, I want fill history so that I can reconcile hosted order executions.
expected_behavior: GET /v0/user/{address}/trades returns historical fills with executed price, size, and settlement tx hash where available.

user_story: As a hosted trader, I want to look up one hosted order so that I can check current status and fill progress.
expected_behavior: GET /v0/orders/{order_id} returns order status, filled and remaining size, average price, and tx hash when settled.

user_story: As a TypeScript developer, I want a typed PMXT client so that I can call local or hosted PMXT methods from Node applications.
expected_behavior: The pmxtjs package exposes exchange classes with camelCase methods, converts response models, chooses hosted or local base URL, and dispatches hosted trading when PMXT API key and wallet configuration require it.

user_story: As a Python developer, I want a Pythonic PMXT client so that I can call the same PMXT features with snake_case methods.
expected_behavior: The pmxt package exposes exchange classes with snake_case methods, converts dataclasses, chooses hosted or local base URL, and dispatches hosted trading where supported.

user_story: As an SDK user, I want to start, stop, restart, and check a local PMXT service so that self-hosted calls work without manual server management.
expected_behavior: Server manager helpers locate or launch the bundled local service and expose status, start, stop, restart, and logs behavior.

user_story: As a hosted trader, I want typed data verified before and after signing so that malformed or tampered orders are rejected client side.
expected_behavior: Hosted signing validates schema, economics, 65-byte signature shape, low-s canonical signatures, v value, and recovered signer.

user_story: As a hosted trader, I want escrow helpers so that I can deposit, withdraw, and inspect escrow lifecycle from the SDK.
expected_behavior: Escrow helper wraps hosted custody operations and uses configured wallet and signer data without hardcoded secrets.

user_story: As a CLI user, I want clear root help and aliases so that I can discover PMXT commands quickly.
expected_behavior: pmxt with no command or help flags renders root help; alias normalization maps curated short names to generated operation commands.
observed_behavior: CLI validation, argv alias normalizer, root help, color, runtime error, feed, enterprise, and production copy verifiers all passed after fixing feed verifier isolation.

user_story: As a CLI user, I want to save and inspect PMXT and venue credentials so that repeated commands can authenticate without flags.
expected_behavior: auth login, logout, status, set-exchange, status-exchange, and remove-exchange manage local credential storage and avoid printing secrets.

user_story: As a CLI user, I want exchange-first data commands so that I can fetch markets, events, order books, trades, balances, and positions from a terminal.
expected_behavior: CLI data commands resolve exchange, hosted or local mode, parameters, output format, and call the matching PMXT operation.

user_story: As a CLI trader, I want order commands so that I can build, create, submit, cancel, and inspect orders from a terminal.
expected_behavior: order and orders commands call buildOrder, createOrder, submitOrder, cancelOrder, fetchOrder, fetchOpenOrders, fetchClosedOrders, fetchAllOrders, and fetchMyTrades as appropriate.

user_story: As a CLI user, I want to manage the local PMXT server so that local mode is operational from the terminal.
expected_behavior: server start, stop, restart, status, health, and logs manage or inspect a local PMXT service.
observed_behavior: CLI validate and production-copy verifiers passed for server command files; real process lifecycle behavior still pending.

user_story: As a CLI user, I want streaming commands so that I can watch order books and trades as JSONL.
expected_behavior: watch commands subscribe over WebSocket, print data events, and handle errors and interrupts cleanly.

user_story: As a CLI user, I want feed commands so that I can fetch Binance and Chainlink feed data from the terminal.
expected_behavior: feed commands map to feed operations for loadMarkets, ticker, tickers, OHLCV, order book, oracle round, oracle history, historical prices, and watch ticker.
test_command: RED: npm test --workspace=@pmxt/cli; GREEN: node scripts/verify-feed-cli.js and npm test --workspace=@pmxt/cli
observed_behavior: Initial CLI suite failed because verify-feed-cli expected --hosted without auth to reject, but the verifier used the real ~/.pmxt auth store. Fixed the verifier to isolate HOME and PMXT_AUTH_STORE_PATH in a temp empty store; full CLI suite now passes.
notes: Logistical test isolation error, not runtime behavior.

user_story: As an enterprise CLI user, I want matched-market and SQL commands so that I can query hosted PMXT data from the terminal.
expected_behavior: enterprise commands expose matched markets, matched prices, and read-only SQL with hosted authentication.
observed_behavior: Enterprise CLI verifier passed hosted flag, custom base URL, env auth, auth-store, query mapping, and deprecated command absence checks.

user_story: As a Dome API user, I want an automated migration helper so that common imports, constructors, methods, parameters, and statuses convert to PMXT equivalents.
expected_behavior: dome-to-pmxt walks TS, JS, and Python files, applies ordered regex transforms, supports --dry-run, skips build and dependency directories, and reports TODOs for manual follow-up.

user_story: As a PMXT user, I want Polymarket support so that I can read and trade Polymarket through the unified API.
expected_behavior: createExchange('polymarket') returns PolymarketExchange with credentials from args or environment and supports the compliance-grid features for Polymarket.

user_story: As a PMXT user, I want Kalshi support so that I can read and trade Kalshi through the unified API.
expected_behavior: createExchange('kalshi') returns KalshiExchange with API key and RSA private key credentials and supports the compliance-grid features for Kalshi.

user_story: As a PMXT user, I want Kalshi demo support so that I can test Kalshi flows against demo infrastructure.
expected_behavior: createExchange('kalshi-demo') returns KalshiDemoExchange using Kalshi credential fields and demo-specific configuration.

user_story: As a PMXT user, I want Limitless support so that I can read and trade Limitless markets through the unified API.
expected_behavior: createExchange('limitless') returns LimitlessExchange with private key and API credential support and compliance-grid features for Limitless.

user_story: As a PMXT user, I want Probable support so that I can access Probable markets through the unified API.
expected_behavior: createExchange('probable') returns ProbableExchange with API key, secret, passphrase, and private key credential support.

user_story: As a PMXT user, I want Baozi support so that I can access Baozi markets through the unified API.
expected_behavior: createExchange('baozi') returns BaoziExchange with private key support and documented emulated or unsupported methods.

user_story: As a PMXT user, I want Myriad support so that I can access Myriad markets through the unified API.
expected_behavior: createExchange('myriad') returns MyriadExchange with API key and wallet-address credential support.

user_story: As a PMXT user, I want Opinion support so that I can access Opinion markets and hosted trading routes through PMXT.
expected_behavior: createExchange('opinion') returns OpinionExchange with API key, private key, and funder address credential support.

user_story: As a PMXT user, I want Metaculus support so that forecasts can be represented through the unified API.
expected_behavior: createExchange('metaculus') returns MetaculusExchange with apiToken support, fetchMarkets and fetchEvents, and forecast create or withdraw behavior mapped to createOrder and cancelOrder.

user_story: As a PMXT user, I want Smarkets support so that I can access Smarkets markets through the unified API.
expected_behavior: createExchange('smarkets') returns SmarketsExchange with email and password credential mapping and Smarkets read and trading methods where implemented.

user_story: As a PMXT user, I want Polymarket US support so that US venue markets can be read through the unified API.
expected_behavior: createExchange('polymarket_us') returns PolymarketUSExchange with key id and secret key credential mapping.

user_story: As a PMXT user, I want Hyperliquid prediction market support so that I can read and trade supported Hyperliquid markets through the unified API.
expected_behavior: createExchange('hyperliquid') returns HyperliquidExchange with wallet address and private key credential mapping.

user_story: As a PMXT user, I want Gemini Titan support so that Gemini prediction markets can be read through PMXT.
expected_behavior: createExchange('gemini-titan') returns GeminiTitanExchange with Gemini API key and secret credential mapping.

user_story: As a PMXT user, I want SuiBets support so that Sui prediction markets can be read through the unified API.
expected_behavior: createExchange('suibets') returns SuiBetsExchange with wallet address and baseUrl credential mapping.

user_story: As a PMXT user, I want Rain support so that Rain markets can be read and traded through the unified API.
expected_behavior: createExchange('rain') returns RainExchange with private key, wallet, subgraph, RPC, and environment configuration.

user_story: As a PMXT user, I want Hunch support so that parimutuel Hunch markets can be represented through the unified API.
expected_behavior: createExchange('hunch') returns HunchExchange, supports keyless reads, x402 market-order createOrder, and documented unsupported cancel or order-fetch behavior.

user_story: As a PMXT developer, I want a mock exchange so that local tests can exercise PMXT behavior without external venue dependencies.
expected_behavior: createExchange('mock') returns MockExchange with deterministic fixtures for local and test use.

user_story: As a data-feed user, I want Binance feed support so that crypto reference prices and order books are available through PMXT feed methods.
expected_behavior: getFeed('binance') returns a singleton BinanceFeed using OBDATA_API_KEY when present and supports feed methods implemented by BinanceFeed.

user_story: As an oracle-data user, I want Chainlink feed support so that oracle rounds and historical oracle prices are available through PMXT feed methods.
expected_behavior: getFeed('chainlink') returns a singleton ChainlinkFeed using OBDATA_API_KEY when present and supports oracle round, oracle history, and historical price methods.
